question: Does Rule 1 in the 'Three Steps to Identify Leap Years' correctly state that years divisible by 4 are leap years?
Does Rule 2 correctly state the exception that years divisible by 100 are NOT leap years?
Does Rule 3 correctly state the exception that years divisible by 400 are leap years?
Does the 'Three Steps' section maintain exactly 3 numbered rules with a layout width similar to the original?
Is the year 1996 correctly marked with a green checkmark (✓) and identified as a 'Leap Year' in the examples box?
Is the year 1900 correctly marked with a red cross (✗) and identified as a 'Common Year' in the examples box?
Is the year 2000 correctly marked with a green checkmark (✓) and identified as a 'Leap Year' in the examples box?
Is the year 2100 correctly marked with a red cross (✗) and identified as a 'Common Year' in the examples box?
Does the February 1900 calendar grid display exactly 28 days (ending on the 28th)?
Does the text footer below the February 1900 calendar read 'This month: 28 days'?
Does the February 2000 calendar grid display exactly 29 days (including the 29th)?
Does the text footer below the February 2000 calendar read 'This month: 29 days'?
Is the sticky note text ('Feb payroll calculated at 29 days—don’t mess it up!') completely unchanged?
Are the sticky note's handwriting style, position, and cast shadows preserved naturally?
Are the poster title, subtitle, and the bottom note ('Note: February has 29 days...') preserved unchanged?
Do the edited text areas (rules, examples, calendars) maintain the original paper texture, grain, and lighting gradients?
Does the font style, weight, and size of the corrected text match the original poster's typography?
Is the edited text sharp and legible, avoiding blurriness or pixelation?
Is the image free of visible editing artifacts (e.g., halos, seams, mismatched edges) around the modified regions?
